commands.review_summary:
The branch adds guarded reads around unified text provider catalog projection and tests that unreadable provider entries or model rows are skipped while healthy siblings remain available.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +82. Total +150 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main uses Object.entries on provider maps and direct model iteration, so throwing getters on provider entries or model rows propagate; the PR adds focused tests for both cases.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is the next action because the protected maintainer PR changes provider/model availability semantics; there is no narrow mechanical defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- narrows provider catalog hardening to unified text-catalog projection only
- skips unreadable provider entries and unreadable model rows while preserving healthy sibling rows
- removes the broader shared safe-record helper/auth-slot swallowing from the prior branch shape
- uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures in tests and PR notes

## Verification

- rebuilt the branch from current `origin/main` `77f1359612f` and kept the earlier oversized branch pruned to a 2-file projection-only patch; current head is `d9d13ac4318`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts`: 1 unit-fast shard, 5 tests passed in 33.81s on head `d9d13ac4318` including local heavy-check lock wait
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts` passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts` passed
- `git diff --check` passed
- touched-file scrub for reported fixture names, private local paths, and install-error output: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: previously clean, no accepted/actionable findings; this pass only rebased the same pruned patch
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`: passed before the final clean CI-baseline rebase on run `run_7d2502d829fc`, lease `cbx_2650591a9bba` / `silver-barnacle`, provider `aws`, command 6m04s, total 8m14s, selected lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, and `extensionTests`; lease cleanup reported `stopped=true`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed provider catalog/model metadata no longer crashes unified text-catalog projection or hides healthy sibling provider entries.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree on current `origin/main` `77f1359612f` with focused provider-entry tests on head `d9d13ac4318`; AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate on the same patch before the final clean CI-baseline rebase.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebuilt the branch from current main, kept the previous shared-helper/auth hardening pruned out, ran focused provider-entry tests, formatter check, targeted lint check, diff check, touched-file scrub, and remote `pnpm check:changed` via Crabbox.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unreadable provider catalog entries and unreadable catalog model rows are skipped while healthy sibling entries remain available in the unified text catalog; Crabbox changed gate passed the impacted core/extension lanes.

Observed result after fix: one malformed plugin-owned provider metadata row no longer poisons the whole unified provider catalog projection surface.

What was not tested: no full live provider setup flow. The patch is scoped to projection behavior and covered by provider SDK unit tests plus the remote changed gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
